top_categories.workout_context: 65 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
101ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt)'s mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 65 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt). Train it until it feels boring.
6,562ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Base fitness for ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt). Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt), that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 6,562ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: May in Eschborn/Frankfurt, Germany can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt). Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt)’s 65 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at ADAC Velotour (Eschborn-Frankfurt). Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 65 miles of mixed terrain in Eschborn/Frankfurt, Germany.
